prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "弓步动态拉伸" (dynamic lunge pulls) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person in a dynamic lunge stretch position: right leg in a deep lunge front position with right foot flat and right knee bent at 90 degrees; left leg extended fully back with knee close to but not touching the ground, ball of left foot on the ground; both hands placed on the floor inside the right foot for support, hips pushed forward and down to deepen the hip flexor stretch on the rear (left) leg; torso upright through the spine, looking forward; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: hip flexors (psoas, rectus femoris) of the rear leg under stretch, and gluteus maximus of the front leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: quadriceps of the rear leg and adductor muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "髂腰肌 / 股直肌" pointing to the hip flexors of the rear leg under stretch; "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus of the front leg; "内收肌群" pointing to the adductor muscles. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
